hypothesis: If language models for materials design are fine-tuned using Direct Preference Optimization guided by physics-based thermodynamic simulation feedback, then they will generate BCC/B2 superalloy compositions with higher reward scores, greater novelty, and better coverage of the valid compositional space compared to supervised fine-tuning alone, few-shot prompting of large API models, and traditional generative baselines, because aligning model outputs to domain-specific physical objectives steers generation away from memorized training distributions toward thermodynamically feasible but previously unexplored alloy compositions.
